metadata.description: Synthetic maintenance logs organized into 3 sequential failure stories for NexusOps AI training data
stories: Story 1: Carrier 30RAP condenser fouling cascade (E3 -> A6 -> E3)
Story 2: York YVAA refrigerant loss and compressor thermal damage (U0 -> E5)
Story 3: Carrier 30RAP low-ambient startup with crankcase heater failure (103 -> E5)

logs.title: E3 High Pressure Trip — First Occurrence
logs.description: Unit 04 went into E3 lockout at 08:23. Ambient temperature was 98°F. Operator reported the unit had been running for 6 hours prior without issue. Manual reset performed without investigation. Unit restarted.
logs.resolution: Manual reset — no root cause identified

logs.title: E3 High Pressure Trip — Second Occurrence Same Day
logs.description: Unit 04 tripped on E3 again only 3 hours after manual reset. Ambient dropped to 94°F but unit still tripped. Technician noted one condenser fan was not rotating during inspection — confirmed A6 fan motor fault was also present but suppressed alarm.
logs.resolution: Fan #3 found stationary. Fan motor overload tripped. Reset overload.
logs.outcome: PARTIAL — overload reset but motor health not verified

logs.title: A6 Fan Motor Fault — Service Investigation
logs.description: Dispatched for active A6 fault on Fan #3. Found run capacitor failed (measured 6µF vs 25µF nameplate rating). Also noted condenser coil heavily fouled — approximately 40% blockage by cottonwood seeds and debris. Both conditions contributing to elevated head pressure.
logs.resolution: Replaced run capacitor (25µF/370VAC). Cleaned condenser coil with Nu-Brite coil cleaner.

logs.title: Post-Repair Verification — Unit Running Stable
logs.description: Performed 2-hour post-repair monitoring run. All 4 condenser fans operating correctly at full amperage. Discharge pressure normalized to 385 psig at 91°F ambient. Subcooling measured at 11°F (within spec). System performance restored to baseline.
logs.resolution: Verification complete — unit operating within specifications

logs.title: Preventive Maintenance — Condenser Inspection Added to PM Schedule
logs.description: Following the May 10-11 failure sequence, condenser coil inspection and cleaning added to monthly PM checklist. All four fan motor capacitors measured and documented as baseline. No faults present.
logs.resolution: Preventive maintenance completed — coil clean, capacitors at spec

logs.title: Monthly PM — Unit Clear, Lessons Learned Documented
logs.description: Monthly PM inspection completed. Condenser coil at 8% blockage after one month — acceptable. All fan motors at nameplate amperage. Recommended increasing PM frequency to monthly during cottonwood season (April-June). Root cause analysis of May failure closed.
logs.resolution: PM complete — no corrective action required

logs.title: U0 Refrigerant Loss — Early Morning Alarm
logs.description: BMS alarm triggered for York YVAA Unit 07 at 06:00. Suction pressure monitoring showed progressive decline over 48-hour period from 178 psig to 118 psig on R-134a system. U0 alarm activated when pressure dropped below 125 psig threshold. Unit continued to operate in degraded mode.
logs.resolution: Alarm acknowledged — leak detection inspection scheduled for next business day
logs.outcome: DEFERRED — high-risk decision to defer service

logs.title: U0 Leak Detection Investigation
logs.description: Arrived on site for U0 investigation. Suction pressure now at 96 psig — significant drop overnight. Performed electronic leak detection scan. Found active leak at left evaporator header brazed joint — detector alarming at >500 ppm. Estimated leak rate ~3 lbs/day based on pressure decay.
logs.resolution: Leak located. Parts ordered for repair. Decision made to continue operating overnight with monitoring.
logs.outcome: PARTIAL — leak found but not repaired; risky continued operation

logs.title: E5 High Discharge Temperature — Critical Alarm
logs.description: E5 fault activated at 23:15 with discharge temperature reaching 248°F — 8°F above the 240°F trip setpoint. Compressor entered thermal protection shutdown. Discharge pressure was 196 psig (low for load conditions, confirming very low charge). Data center cooling load switched to secondary chiller.
logs.resolution: E5 lockout — unit offline. Emergency technician dispatched.

logs.title: Emergency Response — Post E5 Compressor Assessment
logs.description: Emergency technician arrived at 01:30. Unit completely off. Compressor casing temperature at 182°F (still cooling from trip at 23:15). Oil analysis sample taken — will indicate acid formation if compressor valves damaged. Refrigerant recovered (18 lbs R-134a recovered vs 48 lbs nameplate charge — confirms ~30 lbs lost). Evaporator header leak confirmed and repair initiated.
logs.resolution: Brazed joint re-brazed with nitrogen purge. Nitrogen pressure test initiated at 100 psig.

logs.title: Nitrogen Pressure Test Pass — Vacuum and Recharge
logs.description: 24-hour nitrogen pressure test at 100 psig passed — no pressure loss detected. Pulled vacuum to 285 microns over 4 hours. Oil analysis results returned: acid number elevated (0.08 mg KOH/g vs <0.05 limit) — indicates early stage burnout/acid formation. Decision made to replace oil and install acid filter. Recharged with 48 lbs virgin R-134a.
logs.resolution: Oil changed, acid filter installed, recharged to spec. Compressor test run initiated.
logs.outcome: RESOLVED WITH CORRECTIVE ACTION — increased inspection frequency required

logs.title: Post-Repair 24h Monitoring — System Stable
logs.description: 24-hour post-repair monitoring complete. Suction pressure stable at 183 psig (within spec). Discharge temperature at 178°F (well below 240°F limit). Subcooling 12°F, superheat 9°F — system performing correctly. Oil sample retested at 72h: acid number 0.04 (within limit). Root cause analysis completed — additional PM added for quarterly evaporator leak checks.
logs.resolution: Full resolution confirmed. RCA completed and filed.

logs.title: New PM Protocol — Quarterly Refrigerant Leak Survey
logs.description: First quarterly refrigerant leak survey per updated PM protocol. Electronic leak detection scan of all brazed joints and service connections. No leaks detected. Refrigerant charge verified by subcooling method. Sight glass clear. Incident corrective actions verified as sustained.
logs.resolution: PM complete — no issues found

logs.title: 103 Prestart Temperature Alert — Cold Weather Startup
logs.description: Unit 11 attempted startup at 05:30 after overnight setback. Ambient temperature was 28°F — below the 40°F low ambient lockout setting. Controller generated Code 103 and delayed startup. BMS operator attempted remote reset 3 times over 30-minute period. Each reset failed as ambient remained below threshold.
logs.resolution: 103 alarm acknowledged. Facility operator notified. Waiting for ambient rise.
logs.outcome: MONITORING — expected normal behavior at this ambient

logs.title: 103 Investigation — Ambient Now 46°F But Unit Still Failing to Start
logs.description: Technician dispatched when unit failed to start even after ambient reached 46°F (above 40°F lockout threshold). Controller still showing Code 103. Investigation revealed crankcase heater on Compressor Bank A had failed — resistance measured as open circuit (infinite ohms). Compressor oil had not been maintained at proper temperature overnight.
logs.resolution: Crankcase heater failure confirmed. Replacement part ordered.
logs.outcome: PARTIAL — root cause found, repair pending

logs.title: Crankcase Heater Replacement — Temporary Workaround
logs.description: Replacement crankcase heater (240V, 300W) installed per Carrier service manual. Verified heater draws 1.25A at 240VAC (within spec). Oil temperature rising. Elected to wait 8 hours before attempting startup to ensure adequate oil temperature per Carrier startup guideline. External heater blanket applied as supplemental measure.
logs.resolution: Heater replaced. 8-hour pre-heat protocol initiated.

logs.title: E5 High Discharge Temperature on First Startup After 103 — Oil Migration Suspected
logs.description: After 8-hour pre-heat and oil temperature reaching 68°F, unit was started. Within 4 minutes, E5 fault activated. Discharge temperature spiked to 258°F rapidly — faster than typical thermal overload. This behavior consistent with refrigerant-diluted oil (oil migration during extended cold soak — crankcase heater had likely been failing for weeks). Compressor locked out.
logs.resolution: E5 lockout. Suspect oil-refrigerant dilution from extended crankcase heater failure.
logs.outcome: CRITICAL — suspected compressor damage from oil dilution

logs.title: Oil Analysis and Compressor Assessment
logs.description: Oil analysis sample drawn from compressor bank A. Visual inspection of oil: milky/opaque color indicating refrigerant contamination (oil-refrigerant mixture). Suction pressure measured at 92 psig (low for R-410A at 65°F ambient — indicates refrigerant trapped in oil). Compression ratio measured at 2.8 vs expected 4.1 — confirms compressor valve efficiency loss.
logs.resolution: Oil change performed. Extended 16-hour pre-heat protocol initiated at higher oil temperature target (>80°F).
logs.outcome: PARTIAL — compressor efficiency degraded; monitoring required

logs.title: Second Startup Attempt — Partial Success
logs.description: After 16-hour pre-heat with oil temperature at 84°F, unit restarted. No E5 fault on startup. Discharge temperature stabilized at 198°F (below 220°F alarm). However, compression ratio still at 3.2 vs expected 4.1 — efficiency loss of ~22%. Chilled water setpoint raised from 44°F to 48°F to reduce compressor load. Unit running in degraded but stable mode.
logs.resolution: Unit running in degraded mode. Compressor replacement quote requested from Carrier.

logs.title: Carrier Engineering Assessment — Compressor Replacement Required
logs.description: Carrier field engineer assessed Unit 11. Confirmed compressor scroll set damage from oil dilution operation. Compressor bank A to be replaced (remanufactured scroll set available in 3 weeks). Until then, unit operating on Compressor Bank B only at 50% capacity. Crankcase heater interlock relay installed to prevent startup if heater circuit fails.
logs.resolution: Engineering assessment complete. Compressor replacement scheduled.

logs.title: Compressor Bank A Replacement — Full System Restoration
logs.description: Remanufactured scroll compressor bank A installed per Carrier service bulletin. Refrigerant fully recovered (46 lbs), new filter-dryer and oil separator installed. System recharged with virgin R-410A (52 lbs to nameplate). System commissioned at full capacity. Discharge temperature 182°F, compression ratio 4.0 (within spec). Full load test complete.
logs.resolution: Full restoration complete. Unit back at nameplate capacity.

logs.title: Lessons Learned — PM Protocol Updated
logs.description: Post-incident review completed. Root cause traced to crankcase heater failure going undetected for an estimated 4-6 weeks (no heater health monitoring in prior PM). Corrective actions: 1) Monthly crankcase heater resistance check added to winter PM. 2) Startup interlock relay installed. 3) Low ambient startup pre-heat procedure updated to require 80°F oil temp minimum.
lessons_learned: Crankcase heater failure must be detected proactively, not reactively
103 alert in cold weather should always trigger crankcase heater inspection
Oil temperature must be verified before startup, not just ambient temperature
pm_changes_made: Monthly crankcase heater resistance check (target: 1840 ohms ±10%)
Startup interlock relay on heater circuit
logs.resolution: PM protocol updated. Incident closed.

logs.title: E3 Recurrence — Fan #2 Bearing Failure
logs.description: Unit 04 E3 recurrence despite clean coil from June PM. Inspection found Fan #2 motor bearing seized — motor running but at very low RPM (audible grinding). Fan blade visually stationary. Motor amperage on phase 1 was 18.5A vs 4.2A nameplate FLA — motor drawing excessive current through locked rotor.
logs.resolution: Motor overload tripped. Motor replaced (TEFC, 1HP, 460V, 1075RPM). Overload relay reset.

logs.title: Quarterly Refrigerant Survey — Minor Leak Found at Compressor Discharge Service Valve
logs.description: Quarterly refrigerant survey identified minor leak at compressor discharge service valve packing. Leak rate estimated at 0.05 lbs/day via bubble test. Suction pressure trending slightly low (175 psig vs 183 psig baseline) confirming gradual loss. Valve packing replaced during survey.
logs.resolution: Valve packing replaced. Leak test confirmed sealed. No refrigerant addition required.
logs.outcome: RESOLVED — proactive detection prevented major incident

logs.title: Pre-Winter PM — Crankcase Heater Verified, Cold Weather Startup Procedure Reviewed
logs.description: Pre-winter PM completed for Unit 11 ahead of cold season. Crankcase heater resistance measured at 1847 ohms (within 10% of 1840 spec). Startup interlock relay tested — confirmed it prevents startup if heater circuit opens. Cold weather startup procedure reviewed with facility operator. No issues identified.
logs.resolution: Pre-winter PM complete — unit ready for cold weather operations

logs.title: Annual PM — Full Refrigerant System Check
logs.description: Annual PM completed. Refrigerant system check: subcooling 11°F, superheat 9°F, sight glass clear. Condenser coil at 12% blockage — cleaned. All 4 fan motors amperage verified: 4.1A, 4.2A, 4.1A, 4.0A (all within spec). No leaks detected by electronic leak detector. Compressor oil sample sent for analysis.
logs.resolution: Annual PM complete — all systems nominal

logs.title: Annual PM — 18-Month Post-Incident Audit
logs.description: 18-month post-incident audit for YORK-YVAA-UNIT-07. System performing at full capacity. Refrigerant charge verified at nameplate. Oil acid number 0.03 (well within <0.05 limit). No leaks detected across 47 inspection points. Microgateway+ controller event log reviewed — zero refrigerant-related alarms in past 4 months. System declared fully rehabilitated.
logs.resolution: Annual PM complete — system fully rehabilitated
